title: Preserve Python TX queue on radio failure
description: LinkLayer.drain_tx_queue pops before radio.transmit; a False result or exception loses the frame and TxQueue increments transmitted stats before hardware success. Redesign queue acknowledgement/removal so radio failure preserves priority/deadline/order and stats reflect confirmed transmission, with focused False/exception tests.
notes: Implemented radio-confirmed two-phase dequeue, coarse TX serialization, False/exception/cancellation retention, byte-identical retry, confirmed-only stats/latency, own-frame send result, post-CAD expiry recheck, concurrent send/drain serialization, and startup-only sequence restoration preserving frame-byte uniqueness. Link tests 159 passed/4 skipped; Ruff and strict mypy changed sources pass; three direct hash-bound reviews have no in-scope blockers. Entry reservations/high-priority admission tracked 2452; capacity validation tracked separately.
close_reason: Python radio failures preserve exact queued frames and confirmed stats
